title: Discounted Cash Flow: The Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) method does not account for...
summary: Assess whether the DCF method accounts for changes in working capital in cash flow calculations.
prompt: The Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) method does not account for changes in working capital.
solution: The DCF method does take into account changes in working capital as part of cash flow calculations. This ensures that the analysis reflects the company's operational efficiency and liquidity needs.
